---
title: "ECON 444 Consulting Project"
author: "Kendall Landwehr & Tatum Barkat"
format: html
---

```{r}
#Reading in data 

install.packages("readxl")

library(readxl)
consumption_data <- read_excel("data/2021-2025_consumption_data.xlsx")

price_data <- read_excel("data/2021-2025_price_data.xlsx")

#Renaming columns
library(dplyr)

consumption_data <- consumption_data %>%
  rename(Hour_DK = `Time(DK)`)
consumption_data <- consumption_data %>%
  rename(Consumption_kWh = `Consumption(kWh)`)
price_data <- price_data %>%
  rename(Spot_Price_DKK = `Spot Price (DKK)`)
price_data <- price_data %>%
  rename(Hour_DK = `Hour (DK)`)

class(consumption_data$Hour_DK)

#Adjusting time zone data
consumption_data$time <- as.POSIXct(consumption_data$Hour_DK,
                                    format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
                                    tz = "Europe/Copenhagen")

price_data$time <- as.POSIXct(price_data$Hour_DK,
                              format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
                              tz = "Europe/Copenhagen")

#Checks
class(consumption_data$time)
attr(consumption_data$time, "tzone")
```

```{r}
#Merging data sets

library(dplyr)

merged_data <- left_join(consumption_data,
                         price_data,
                         by = c("time", "Region"))
#Deleting unnecessary hour columns

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(-Hour_DK.x, -Hour_DK.y)

#Renaming time column and moving to left
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  rename (Time = time)
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(Time, everything())
```

```{r}
# Fit the regression model
model <- lm(Consumption_kWh ~ Spot_Price_DKK, data = merged_data)

# View a summary of results
summary(model)

# Plot residuals vs fitted values
plot(model, which = 1)
```


```{r}
# Normal Q-Q plot to check residuals
plot(model, which = 2)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK, y = Consumption_kWh)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Spot Price (DKK)", y = "Consumption (kWh)",
       title = "Consumption vs Spot Price")
```

```{r}
#Checking to see if there's other things that could be making the regression so awful
sum(is.na(merged_data$Spot_Price_DKK))
unique(consumption_data$region)
unique(price_data$Region)
summary(merged_data$Consumption_kWh)
summary(merged_data$Spot_Price_DKK)

```
```{r}
###ChatGPT Log-Transform Regression Attempt
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1️⃣ Backup original dataset
merged_data_backup <- merged_data  # keeps the original intact

# 2️⃣ Inspect price distribution
summary(merged_data$Spot_Price_DKK)

# 3️⃣ Shift Spot_Price_DKK so all values are positive (preserves negative prices)
min_price <- min(merged_data$Spot_Price_DKK)
merged_data_clean <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    Spot_Price_DKK_adj = Spot_Price_DKK + abs(min_price) + 1  # shift to >0
  )

# 4️⃣ Log-transform consumption and adjusted price
merged_data_clean <- merged_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    log_Consumption = log(Consumption_kWh),
    log_Price = log(Spot_Price_DKK_adj)
  )

# 5️⃣ Run log-log regression
log_model <- lm(log_Consumption ~ log_Price, data = merged_data_clean)

# 6️⃣ View regression summary
summary(log_model)

# 7️⃣ Add predicted log-consumption values
merged_data_clean <- merged_data_clean %>%
  mutate(predicted_log_Consumption = predict(log_model))

# 8️⃣ Optional: plot log-log relationship
ggplot(merged_data_clean, aes(x = log_Price, y = log_Consumption)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "blue") +
  labs(
    x = "Log Spot Price (DKK, shifted)",
    y = "Log Consumption (kWh)",
    title = "Log-Log Regression: Consumption vs Price"
  )

# 🔹 If you ever want to revert back to original data:
# merged_data <- merged_data_backup

##Shows slightly more of a relationship but still very small correlation...
```
```{r}
#Adding Region and Sector as Factors to the Log-transformed regression model

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1️⃣ Backup the original data (if not already done)
merged_data_backup <- merged_data

# 2️⃣ Shift Spot_Price_DKK to handle negative values
min_price <- min(merged_data$Spot_Price_DKK)
merged_data_clean <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    Spot_Price_DKK_adj = Spot_Price_DKK + abs(min_price) + 1,  # shift > 0
    log_Consumption = log(Consumption_kWh),
    log_Price = log(Spot_Price_DKK_adj),
    Region = factor(Region),   # ensure categorical
    Sector = factor(Sector)    # ensure categorical
  )

# 3️⃣ Run log-log regression with Region and Sector as factors
log_model_factors <- lm(
  log_Consumption ~ log_Price + Region + Sector,
  data = merged_data_clean
)

# 4️⃣ View regression summary
summary(log_model_factors)

# 5️⃣ Add predicted values to the dataset
merged_data_clean <- merged_data_clean %>%
  mutate(predicted_log_Consumption = predict(log_model_factors))

# 6️⃣ Optional: Plot consumption vs price by sector
ggplot(merged_data_clean, aes(x = log_Price, y = log_Consumption, color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Log Spot Price (DKK, shifted)",
    y = "Log Consumption (kWh)",
    title = "Log-Log Regression: Consumption vs Price by Sector"
  )

# 🔹 To revert to original data anytime:
# merged_data <- merged_data_backup

##Interpretation: Region and Sector dominate consumption patterns, while price plays a small but significant role.
```
```{r}
#Model to show relationship between price and sector elasticity

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1️⃣ Ensure we’re starting from the clean dataset
# (with shifted prices, log-transformed consumption and price, and factors for Region/Sector)
merged_data_interact <- merged_data_clean

# 2️⃣ Run regression with interaction between log_Price and Sector
log_model_interact <- lm(
  log_Consumption ~ log_Price * Sector + Region,   # interaction included
  data = merged_data_interact
)

# 3️⃣ View summary
summary(log_model_interact)

# 4️⃣ Add predicted values for plotting
merged_data_interact <- merged_data_interact %>%
  mutate(predicted_log_Consumption = predict(log_model_interact))

# 5️⃣ Plot predicted relationship by Sector
ggplot(merged_data_interact, aes(x = log_Price, y = log_Consumption, color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Log Spot Price (DKK, shifted)",
    y = "Log Consumption (kWh)",
    title = "Log-Log Regression with Price x Sector Interaction"
  )
```

```{r}
#Trying removing negative and zeros to get clearer regression

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1️⃣ Start from the clean dataset with shifted prices and log-transformed columns
merged_data_positive <- merged_data_clean

# 2️⃣ Remove rows where consumption or price is negative
merged_data_positive <- merged_data_positive %>%
  filter(Consumption_kWh > 0, Spot_Price_DKK > 0)

# 3️⃣ Run regression with interaction again
log_model_positive <- lm(
  log_Consumption ~ log_Price * Sector + Region,
  data = merged_data_positive
)

# 4️⃣ View summary
summary(log_model_positive)

# 5️⃣ Optional: Plot the cleaned relationship
ggplot(merged_data_positive, aes(x = log_Price, y = log_Consumption, color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Log Spot Price (DKK, shifted)",
    y = "Log Consumption (kWh)",
    title = "Log-Log Regression (Negative Prices/Consumption Removed)"
  )

# 🔹 Backup reminder: original data is still in merged_data_backup


##Plot of relationship

library(ggplot2)

# Plot log-consumption vs log-price by sector
ggplot(merged_data_positive, aes(x = log_Price, y = log_Consumption, color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +   # semi-transparent points to show density
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 1.2) +  # regression line for each sector
  labs(
    title = "Log-Log Regression of Energy Consumption vs Spot Price by Sector",
    x = "Log Spot Price (DKK, shifted)",
    y = "Log Consumption (kWh)",
    color = "Sector"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
